skill.name: qwen-image-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0 models through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Create Qwen-Image-2.0 images and edits with infsh commands.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and URL patterns, but the reviewed file is a documentation skill that calls the inference.sh CLI for its intended image workflow. The high weak-cryptography alerts appear to be false positives from model names, version-like text, and option tables, with no cryptographic implementation found. Publication is acceptable with warnings because prompts and reference image URLs are sent to an external inference service through CLI commands.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Executes Cloud Image Jobs
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access to infsh and repeatedly instructs users to run infsh app commands. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it executes an external CLI and sends prompts or image references to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The frontmatter explicitly allows Bash(infsh *), and examples show infsh app run commands. The risk is confirmed, but it matches the stated image generation purpose.

medium_findings.title: External Network Service and Remote Image URLs
medium_findings.description: The workflow depends on inference.sh documentation and cloud execution, and the image editing example accepts remote reference image URLs. Users may expose sensitive prompts or image URLs to the external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text contains explicit inference.sh links and reference_images URI fields. There is no evidence of covert exfiltration, but external data transfer is part of normal operation.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations correspond to descriptive text, model names, option tables, or metadata fields. No hashing, encryption, signing, or insecure cryptographic API usage was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation content at the flagged lines, not cryptographic code. The static pattern likely matched version-like or table text.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged line describes the negative_prompt image-generation parameter. It does not collect host information or run reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is an input option description for content to avoid in generated images. No system command or host inspection behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Require Safe Quoting
low_findings.description: The command examples embed JSON inside shell arguments. This is normal CLI documentation, but agents should escape user-supplied prompt text before placing it in shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples use static prompts and are not directly vulnerable as written. Risk appears if an assistant interpolates unescaped user text into the shell command.

dangerous_patterns.title: Permitted Shell Command Prefix
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill permits Bash commands beginning with infsh. This should stay limited to the documented inference.sh CLI workflow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration is explicit. The scope is limited to infsh, which lowers but does not remove external command risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Cloud Inference Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented infsh app run commands call Alibaba Qwen image apps through inference.sh, creating expected outbound network activity.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are concrete and tied to remote app IDs. No hidden destination was found beyond the documented service.

content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable way to generate and edit images with Qwen-Image models. This skill provides ready infsh commands for text-to-image, reference edits, sizing, seeds, and text rendering.
actual_capabilities: Runs Qwen-Image-2.0 and Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through infsh app commands.
Provides examples for basic text-to-image generation and multiple output images.
Shows how to set width, height, seed, negative prompt, and prompt extension options.
Supports multi-reference image editing with one to three reference image URIs.
Compares the fast and Pro models for text rendering, realism, and semantic adherence.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated inference.sh account.
Sends prompts and reference image locations to an external cloud service.
Does not include local model execution or offline image generation.
Exact text rendering quality depends on the selected model and prompt clarity.

use_cases.title: Generate Campaign Concepts
use_cases.description: Create several visual directions for ads, posters, or social media assets from short creative prompts.

use_cases.title: Edit With Reference Images
use_cases.description: Combine subject, clothing, pose, or layout references into a new edited image using remote image URIs.

use_cases.title: Prototype Text-Heavy Visuals
use_cases.description: Use the Pro model for posters, sale graphics, and layouts where exact visible text matters.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Image Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Create one simple image from a plain-language idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-2 to generate an image of [subject] in [style] with [lighting]. Keep the prompt concise and use default settings.

prompt_templates.title: Sized Concept Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate multiple options at a specific resolution.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate [number] images with qwen-image-2 for [concept]. Use width [pixels], height [pixels], and include a short negative prompt for unwanted artifacts.

prompt_templates.title: Text Poster With Pro Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Create an image where visible text must be accurate.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-2-pro to create a poster with the exact title "[title]" and subtitle "[subtitle]". Set prompt_extend to false and specify text position, color, and style.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Blend multiple references into one edited result.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-2 to edit reference images [image 1 URL], [image 2 URL], and [image 3 URL]. Describe how each reference should influence the final image.

output_examples.input: A request for four minimalist coffee shop logo concepts.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares an infsh command using qwen-image-2, sets num_images to four, and describes where the generated PNG outputs will appear.

output_examples.input: A poster request with exact sale text and a vertical layout.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects qwen-image-2-pro, disables prompt extension, sets a portrait resolution, and includes the exact headline and subtitle in the prompt.

output_examples.input: A user wants to edit a person image using clothing and pose references.
output_examples.output: The assistant builds a multi-reference edit command with three image URIs and explains that the images are sent to the external inference service.

best_practices: Use the Pro model when readable text is more important than speed.
Keep prompts specific about subject, style, composition, lighting, and unwanted details.
Review prompts and reference image URLs for sensitive information before running infsh.
anti_patterns: Do not paste confidential prompts, private images, or restricted URLs into cloud inference commands.
Do not interpolate unescaped user text directly into shell commands.
Do not expect offline generation, because the skill depends on inference.sh services.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It provides infsh commands for generating and editing images with Qwen-Image-2.0 models.

faq.question: Does it require an account?
faq.answer: Yes. The quick start shows infsh login, so users need access to inference.sh.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents reference_images for one to three input image URIs.

faq.question: When should I use the Pro model?
faq.answer: Use the Pro model for posters, text-heavy images, and cases that need better text rendering.

faq.question: What security warning should users know?
faq.answer: Prompts and reference image URLs may be sent to an external cloud service through infsh.
